steps.title: Use a weighted mean
steps.content: The two penguin groups have different sizes, so do not average $87$ and $93$ directly. Weight each mean by the number of penguins in that group.

steps.title: Calculate the total height sum
steps.content: The Kerguelen group contributes $$380\cdot87=33{,}060.$$ The Falkland group contributes $$190\cdot93=17{,}670.$$ The combined total is $$33{,}060+17{,}670=50{,}730.$$

steps.title: Divide by the total number of penguins
steps.content: There are $$380+190=570$$ penguins total, so the overall mean is $$\frac{50{,}730}{570}=89.$$
